title: The people behind the papers - Justina Stark, Ivo Sbalzarini and Michael Brand.
abstract: Morphogens are secreted from a local source and form long-range gradients. Existing computational models to study morphogen gradients typically simplify the tissue geometry. In a new study, Justina Stark and colleagues investigate the contribution of the porous 3D tissue geometry to long-range Fgf8a gradients in the early zebrafish embryo. To learn more about the people behind this work, we caught up with first author Justina Stark, and corresponding authors Ivo Sbalzarini (Professor at TU Dresden and Group Leader at the Max Planck Institute of Molecular Cell Biology and Genetics) and Michael Brand (Professor at the Center for Regenerative Therapies, TU Dresden).
